privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic water leak detection task using 30 days of household water meter readings (Jan 1-30, 2024). Key verified facts: data is fully synthetic, created by setup script with hardcoded CSV; task uses 7-day rolling average baseline to detect anomalies; threshold is 150% of baseline (baseline × 1.5); excess water calculated as (usage - baseline) only when usage exceeds threshold; only 4 anomaly days detected with this data (Jan 8, 14, 18, 29 with 189, 172, 197, 207 gallons respectively), totaling 346.6 gallons excess at $0.01/gallon = $3.47 cost; cost per gallon is $0.01 per README; data includes intentional meter outage on Jan 11 (0 gallons); anomaly days cluster around normal baseline of ~99-101 gal/day. NOTE: setup_task.sh claims 5-8 expected leak days but actual data with 150% threshold yields only 4 days, contradicting that claim. README and verifier specify 4-9 days which matches actual result. Verifier code checks 150-800 gallons excess but feedback message says 200-600 gallons.
